references.text:
[EFKT92] Erd\H{o}s, P. and Fon-Der-Flaass, D. and Kostochka, A. V. and
Tuza, Zs., Small transversals in uniform hypergraphs. Siberian Adv. Math. (1992), 82-88.

remarks.text: A problem of Erd\H{o}s, Fon-Der-Flaass, Kostochka, and Tuza \cite{EFKT92}, who proved that $f(k,3)=2k$ and $f(k,4)=\lfloor 3k/2\rfloor$ and $f(k,5)=\lfloor 5k/4\rfloor$, and further that $f(k,6)=k$.

statement.text: Let $f(k,r)$ be minimal such that if $A_1,A_2,\ldots$ is a family of sets, all of size $k$, such that for every collection of $r$ of the $A_is$ there is some pair $\{x,y\}$ which intersects all of the $A_j$, then there is some set of size $f(k,r)$ which intersects all of the sets $A_i$. Is it true that\[f(k,7)=(1+o(1))\frac{3}{4}k?\]Is it true that for any $r\geq 3$ there exists some constant $c_r$ such that\[f(k,r)=(1+o(1))c_rk?\]
